Explanation: Using `selectFrom(table)` fetches all columns from the table, which can lead to unnecessary data transfer and maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Consider explicitly specifying only the columns needed, e.g., `dsl.select(DSL.field("col_c1"), DSL.field("col_c2")).from(this.table)`.

Using `selectFrom(demo)` fetches all columns from the table, which can lead to unnecessary data transfer and performance issues. Consider explicitly specifying only the required columns using `select(column1, column2).from(demo)`.

Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the 'demo' table (SELECT *), which retrieves unnecessary data and can cause performance issues. Replace with an explicit column selection like dsl.select(specificColumns).from(DSL.table("demo")).
